name: Holy Lance
description: The Holy Lance, also known as the Lance of Longinus, Spear of Destiny, or Holy Spear, is a legendary relic of immense significance in Christian tradition. It is believed to be the spear that pierced the side of Jesus Christ during his crucifixion, as described in the Gospel of John (19:34). The lance is said to have been wielded by the Roman centurion Longinus. Possession of the Holy Lance has been associated with power and destiny throughout history. Legend states that whoever possesses the lance controls the fate of the world, while loss of the lance brings about downfall. Numerous historical figures, including Roman emperors, Charlemagne, and Adolf Hitler, have sought to possess the lance, believing it would grant them invincibility and power. The lance has been housed in various locations throughout history, including Jerusalem, Antioch, Constantinople, and Vienna. Its authenticity remains a subject of debate, with multiple purported lances existing. The power attributed to the lance stems from its connection to the blood of Christ, imbuing it with divine energy and the potential to influence historical events and individual destinies. It remains a powerful symbol of faith, sacrifice, and the intersection of the earthly and divine realms.
subtitle: Pierced the side of Christ.
origin: Used to pierce the side of Jesus Christ during the Crucifixion

keyMyths.title: The Piercing
keyMyths.description: The Roman soldier Longinus pierced Jesus' side with the lance to confirm his death. Upon contact with the blood, Longinus' failing eyesight was restored, leading to his conversion.

keyMyths.title: The Power of Possession
keyMyths.description: Legend claims that whoever possesses the Holy Lance wields the fate of the world. Its loss is said to bring about ruin and defeat to its holder.

sources.author: Gospel of John
sources.description: Account of the crucifixion and the piercing of Jesus' side.
sources.text: Account of the crucifixion and the piercing of Jesus' side.

sources.author: Trevor Ravenscroft
sources.description: A controversial account linking the lance to historical figures and occult beliefs.
